text.title: Help and Documentation Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Help and Documentation. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Help pages are task-focused (How do I X?) — not feature-focused (What does this button do?).
rule.rationale: Help pages are task-focused (How do I X?) — not feature-focused (What does this button do?).

rule.statement: Every error state and key UI surface has a contextual help link.
rule.rationale: Every error state and key UI surface has a contextual help link.

rule.statement: Help has full-text search with ≤2s latency and answers in the top 3 hits.
rule.rationale: Help has full-text search with ≤2s latency and answers in the top 3 hits.

rule.statement: Pages carry last_reviewed date; pages older than 12 months are flagged.
rule.rationale: Pages carry last_reviewed date; pages older than 12 months are flagged.

rule.statement: Deflection rate is tracked per page; pages below threshold are rewritten or retired.
rule.rationale: Deflection rate is tracked per page; pages below threshold are rewritten or retired.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-help-documentation.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
